bridge_note.indication: Ground-level fall on anticoagulation; new confusion. Rule out intracranial hemorrhage.
bridge_note.narrative: 71M on apixaban for atrial fibrillation, found down after a witnessed ground-level fall with brief loss of consciousness. GCS 13 on arrival, now 14, with a new right parietal scalp hematoma. Evaluate for acute intracranial hemorrhage.

tech_sheet.description: CT head without contrast. Helical acquisition, 5.0 mm axial reconstructions, brain and bone windows.

pacs.prior_description: Comparison to CT head dated 2025-11-02, which showed age-appropriate involutional change and no acute intracranial hemorrhage.

radiology_model.findings:
Extra-axial spaces: Crescentic hyperdense collection along the left cerebral convexity measuring up to 11 mm, consistent with an acute subdural hematoma. Effacement of the adjacent sulci.
Ventricles/midline: Rightward midline shift of approximately 6 mm at the level of the septum pellucidum, with partial effacement of the left lateral ventricle.
Parenchyma: No large territorial infarct. Grey-white differentiation otherwise preserved.
Bone: No displaced calvarial fracture.
radiology_model.impression: Acute left convexity subdural hematoma up to 11 mm with 6 mm of rightward midline shift and early left lateral ventricular effacement. Findings are new since the prior study.

radiologist_dictation: Confirm acute left convexity subdural hematoma with 6 mm midline shift. Given anticoagulation, this warrants immediate neurosurgical notification and reversal consideration.
